---
description: "Use when writing or editing documentation: README, client docs, markdown files, project notes. Covers bilingual format, client communication tone, and documentation structure."
applyTo: ["docs/**", "*.md"]
---
# Documentation Guidelines

## Language
- All documentation must be bilingual EN/ES or in the language appropriate for the audience
- Client-facing docs: primary language of the client
- Technical docs: English primary with key terms in both languages

## Client Communication Tone
- Direct, technically accessible
- Professional but approachable
- Use data and metrics to support recommendations
- Avoid unnecessary jargon — explain technical terms when used

## Documentation Structure
- `docs/README.md` — Technical project documentation
- `docs/cliente/` — Client-facing deliverables and reports
- `README.md` — Project root overview for developers

## Client Deliverables in `docs/cliente/`
- Methodology, audits, briefs, project plans, checklists
- Monthly reports, communication scripts
- Photography guide, website copy
- Lead tracker, dashboard architecture

## Markdown Formatting
- Use tables for structured data
- Include code blocks with language identifiers
- Use relative links between docs
- Include last-updated dates on client deliverables
